hypothesis: If the CNN-GRU-Attention model trained on coarse-grained binary bead sequences is extended to encode polymer sequences as continuous physicochemical property vectors (e.g., hydrophobicity, charge, hydrogen-bonding capacity) rather than binary strong/weak labels, then the model will capture non-monotonic, chemistry-specific binding free energy landscapes that cannot be predicted by linear interpolation between the binary extremes, enabling accurate inverse design of multi-component heteropolymers with targeted adhesion selectivity across chemically distinct surface functionalities.
